frameworks.name: type-2 signal detection / ROC analysis
frameworks.one_line: ROC framework for relating an internal signal to response correctness

frameworks.name: two-stage dynamic signal detection model
frameworks.one_line: dynamic SDT account simulated to reproduce the observed RT-confidence patterns

core_question: Can response time substitute for or complement confidence in type-2 ROC analyses of perceptual decisions?
key_assumptions: type-2 ROC validly indexes the link between an internal variable and response correctness
RT and confidence both carry metacognitive information about perceptual decisions
manipulations: compared RT-based vs confidence-based type-2 ROC across 16 perceptual decision-making datasets
examined RT-confidence interaction by conditioning on short vs longer RTs
key_findings: RT carries roughly two-thirds as much information about correctness as confidence does
RT predicts correctness uniquely, beyond what confidence predicts
confidence becomes a stronger predictor of correctness when RT is short
subject-level meta-d' from RT and from confidence correlate reasonably well
interpretations: RT is a viable low-cost proxy for confidence when confidence is unavailable
RT and confidence combined give complementary metacognitive insight, consistent with two-stage dynamic SDT
